heroHeadline: Somalia: SNA Mobilizes Counter-Offensive to Retake Adan Yabal as Constitutional Crisis Enters Final 24 Days; 6.5M Face Acute Hunger
heroSubtitle: Al-Shabaab's April 19 seizure of Adan Yabal in Hiiraan Region — killing 8 civilians — triggered an emergency SNA-Macawisley counter-offensive mobilization as of April 21. The seizure exploits Somalia's deepening constitutional vacuum: Parliament's mandate expired April 14 without elections, and President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud's term expires May 15 (24 days away) with no electoral framework agreed. Simultaneously, UN agencies confirmed on April 21 that 6.5 million Somalis face acute hunger — one-third of the population — with the $852M Humanitarian Response Plan only 13.4% funded and WFP warning food aid could halt entirely this month. AFRICOM has conducted 56 confirmed Somalia airstrikes in 2026 (through April 17), on pace to surpass 200 annual strikes, while the Jubbaland theater saw 81 Al-Shabaab fighters killed (54 in airstrikes, 27 in ground ops) during the April 15-18 offensive. Somalia faces its most dangerous convergence of governance failure, security deterioration, and humanitarian catastrophe since 2021.
footerNote: Data sourced from ACLED, OCHA, UNSOM, AFRICOM, UN Monitoring Group, and major international outlets. Casualty figures contested where noted.
